section.p: # source_id: cumbia # title: Cumbia # source_url: https://en.wikipedia.org/w/api.php?action=query&prop=extracts&explaintext=1&format=json&titles=Cumbia # license: CC BY-SA 4.0 # attribution: Wikipedia

figure.name: Ingested Figure

step.action: Cumbia refers to a number of musical rhythms and

figure.step: Cumbia refers to a number of musical rhythms and folk dance traditions of Latin America, generally involving musical and cultural elements from American Indigenous peoples, Europeans, and Africans during colonial times.

step.action: Cumbia traditionally uses three drums (tambora,

figure.step: Cumbia traditionally uses three drums (tambora, tambor alegre and llamador), three flutes (gaita hembra and gaito macho, both forms of Colombian flute, and flauta de millo) and has a 22 or 24 meter.

step.action: The sound of cumbia can be characterized as havi

figure.step: The sound of cumbia can be characterized as having a simple "chu-chucu-chu" rhythm created by the guacharaca.

step.action: Examples of cumbia include: Colombian cumbia is

figure.step: Examples of cumbia include: Colombian cumbia is a musical rhythm and traditional folk dance from Colombia.

step.action: The Colombian cumbia is the origin of all the ot

figure.step: The Colombian cumbia is the origin of all the other variations, including the tradition of dancing it with candles in the dancers' hands.

step.action: Panamanian cumbia, Panamanian folk dance and mus

figure.step: Panamanian cumbia, Panamanian folk dance and musical genre, developed by enslaved people of African descent during colonial times and later syncretized with American Indigenous and European cultural elements.

step.action: As it enters new regions, musicians incorporate

figure.step: As it enters new regions, musicians incorporate local instruments, rhythms, and performance practices while retaining the genre's recognizable percussion structure.

step.action: To be more specific, its dance came from a coast

figure.step: To be more specific, its dance came from a coastal traditional culture, as cumbia had multiple ethnic influences that originated from this region.

step.action: The influence came from the costeño dance.

figure.step: The influence came from the costeño dance.

step.action: The history of cumbia has evolved throughout the

figure.step: The history of cumbia has evolved throughout the years, known as a street dance but had a period of transiting into a ballroom dance.

step.action: Cumbia is commonly known for having many subgenr

figure.step: Cumbia is commonly known for having many subgenres from different countries which contributes to the different dance styles known.

step.action: Cumbia can be referred to as a folk dance while

figure.step: Cumbia can be referred to as a folk dance while also being known globally as a street dance.

step.action: To better understand what the dances of cumbia r

figure.step: To better understand what the dances of cumbia resemble it's better to know the basics of the dance.

step.action: This is crucial since the dance from the Atlanti

figure.step: This is crucial since the dance from the Atlantic coast has the woman holding a candle in her right hand.

step.action: This serves as two narrative functions; one to l

figure.step: This serves as two narrative functions; one to light the way for the dancing woman and the latter for a more serious motif.

step.action: The latter can be portrayed in an imaginative se

figure.step: The latter can be portrayed in an imaginative sentence as a weapon by which the woman defends herself against the advances of her partner.

step.action: Since the 1950s, cumbia has been an art form tha

figure.step: Since the 1950s, cumbia has been an art form that is stylized, orchestrated and lyricized, contrary to the traditional form.

step.action: The transformation of cumbia in other countries

figure.step: The transformation of cumbia in other countries to better align with the taste of populations with very different aesthetic traditions from the strongly African-derived coastal culture from which it originally emerged.

step.action: Cumbia is said to have come from funeral traditi

figure.step: Cumbia is said to have come from funeral traditions in the Afro-Colombian community.

step.action: The genre frequently incorporates brass instrume

figure.step: The genre frequently incorporates brass instruments and piano.

step.action: In order to properly understand the interlocking

figure.step: In order to properly understand the interlocking relationship between cumbia's roots, its Pan-American (and then global) roots, and its subgenres, Colombia's geocultural complexities must be taken into account.

step.action: It has elements of three different cultures, Ame

figure.step: It has elements of three different cultures, American Indigenous, African, and Spanish, being the result of the long and intense meeting of these cultures during the Conquest and the Colony.

step.action: Over the 20th century, Cumbia expanded beyond Co

figure.step: Over the 20th century, Cumbia expanded beyond Colombia through migration, media circulation, and cross border musical exchange.

step.action: In Mexico, Cumbia developed into multiple sub ge

figure.step: In Mexico, Cumbia developed into multiple sub genres, becoming a cornerstone of both urban popular culture and Mexican American community identity.
